stage.name: Dual-Host Local-First MVP
stage.targetUser: Solo and small-team game developers who need zero-install access or convenient local file workflows without a hosted account service
stage.qualityGate: Every new or changed task flow passes through the actual UI on its declared host, every affected baseline flow passes regression, and cross-host project and output parity is exact

promises.statement: A developer can open the GitHub Pages product without installation, manage ordinary projects locally, and continue without an application server.
promises.user: A developer who wants immediate browser access
promises.context: Using the published GitHub Pages application on a supported browser
promises.trigger: They need to create or continue sprite work
promises.outcome: Projects are listed, opened, autosaved, recovered, and backed up locally through IndexedDB and portable archives
promises.quality: Ordinary project work survives browser restart, exposes storage status, and never depends on paid infrastructure
promises.recovery: The user can restore snapshots or import a project archive when browser state is damaged or cleared
promises.evidence: Web project-library, persistence, backup, offline, and data-custody flows pass on the published-subpath build

promises.statement: A developer can run a portable Electron edition locally and use ordinary project folders and export folders without repetitive browser downloads.
promises.user: A developer who prefers direct local file operations
promises.context: Using the portable Windows x64 Electron ZIP without an installer
promises.trigger: They need durable folders, direct saving, or repeated engine exports
promises.outcome: The user creates or opens a project folder, saves atomically, recovers safely, and writes generic or Godot output into a selected directory
promises.quality: The portable host requires no administrator rights and exposes only narrow user-approved filesystem operations
promises.recovery: Save conflicts, missing folders, external changes, and unsaved close attempts fail visibly without losing the last known-good project
promises.evidence: Portable launch, folder lifecycle, direct export, and unsaved-close flows pass in packaged Electron

promises.statement: A developer can move the same project between web and desktop without changing its creative meaning, provenance, or generated output.
promises.user: A developer alternating between browser convenience and desktop file access
promises.context: Transferring a versioned .spriteproject archive or opening an equivalent project folder
promises.outcome: Both hosts load the same project schema, pack locks, recipes, themes, credits, and export contract
promises.quality: Semantic project data, render hashes, credits, and engine metadata remain identical across hosts
promises.recovery: Unsupported versions and missing packs fail closed with an intact source archive and actionable resolution
promises.evidence: Web-to-desktop and desktop-to-web round trips pass with exact parity assertions

promises.statement: A developer retains custody of project and art data without an account or silent upload.
promises.user: A developer responsible for preserving creative work
promises.trigger: They inspect storage, back up work, or choose where files are written
promises.outcome: The product states where data lives, provides portable backup, and performs no undeclared project-data transfer
promises.quality: Storage and destination status are visible before destructive or external operations
promises.recovery: A user-owned archive or project folder restores work independently of one browser profile or app session
promises.evidence: Data-custody and cross-host flows pass with zero undeclared network or filesystem activity

scenarios.startingState: GitHub Pages app loaded with no IndexedDB projects
scenarios.intent: Create and retain a first project
scenarios.success: Project appears in the library and survives reload

scenarios.intent: Find, open, rename, or remove a project
scenarios.success: The selected operation affects only the intended project

scenarios.intent: Create a portable backup and restore it after storage loss
scenarios.environment: Browser with download and file input support
scenarios.success: Imported project reproduces the original semantic and render state

scenarios.startingState: App and required packs loaded once online
scenarios.intent: Continue ordinary work without network access
scenarios.success: Installed projects and packs remain usable and exports download locally

scenarios.intent: Launch without installation or administrator rights
scenarios.success: The app opens and identifies the desktop host and available file capabilities

scenarios.intent: Create, open, save, and relocate a project folder
scenarios.success: Canonical files are written atomically and reopen with the same render

scenarios.intent: Write engine files directly to a selected directory
scenarios.success: Expected generic or Godot files appear with matching hashes

scenarios.intent: Choose whether to save, discard, or continue editing
scenarios.success: The exact selected action occurs without accidental loss

scenarios.environment: Web archive and desktop project-folder adapters
scenarios.success: Project, packs, preview, credits, and exports are semantically identical

scenarios.intent: Understand where data lives and create a user-owned copy
scenarios.success: Data location, persistence, destination, and backup action are explicit

capabilities.statement: User can open the production app from GitHub Pages without an account or installation.
User can create a project in the IndexedDB-backed library.
User can list local projects with name, update time, pack state, and recovery status.
User can open one project from the browser library without affecting others.
User can rename a browser project while preserving its stable ID and content.
User can delete one browser project only after explicit confirmation.
User can rely on transactional IndexedDB autosave with visible status and snapshots.
User can reload or restart the browser and continue the exact saved project state.
User can download a versioned .spriteproject backup.
User can inspect and import a valid .spriteproject archive with explicit conflict handling.
User can inspect approximate usage, quota, persistence state, and disposable cache choices.
User can complete installed project workflows offline after the first successful load.
User can launch the portable Windows x64 Electron app without installation or administrator rights.
User can see that the desktop host and local file capabilities are active.
User can create a canonical project folder in a chosen location.
User can open a compatible project folder and see validation before editing.
User can save canonical files atomically with visible dirty, saving, saved, and failed states.
User can relocate a project through Save As without silently changing its semantic identity.
User can open a recent valid project folder and remove stale entries.
User can write generic or Godot output directly into a selected directory.
User can choose Save, Discard, or Cancel when closing a dirty project.
User can export a web project archive and continue it in Electron.
User can export a desktop project archive and continue it in the web app.
User receives identical semantic project data, render hashes, credits, and export metadata from either host.
User can see whether project data lives in IndexedDB, a project folder, or a selected export destination.

navigation.label: Projects
navigation.backBehavior: Returns to the previously open project without discarding edits

navigation.label: Workspace
navigation.backBehavior: Returns to the current creative view

navigation.label: Export
navigation.backBehavior: No output is written until commanded

navigation.label: File
navigation.backBehavior: Native dialog cancellation leaves the project unchanged

views.name: Host start
views.purpose: Identify host capabilities and offer appropriate entry actions

views.name: Creative workspace
views.purpose: Retain the verified compose, theme, preview, and export workflow

views.name: Storage and backup
views.purpose: Explain data custody, manage archives, and expose recovery state

views.name: Archive import
views.purpose: Inspect, validate, migrate, and resolve archive conflicts before commit

views.name: Export
views.purpose: Generate downloads or direct desktop directory output

views.name: Desktop file operations
views.purpose: Create, open, save, relocate, and archive local project folders

views.name: Unsaved changes confirmation
views.purpose: Resolve Save, Discard, or Cancel before close

expectedBehaviors.oracle: Projects view lists exactly the IndexedDB projects ordered by updated time and shows name, pack state, save state, and last update.
New project commits one valid IndexedDB record and adds one selected library item without changing existing projects.
Open project loads the selected stable ID and exact render while every other project remains unchanged.
Rename changes only the project name, preserves stable ID and render hash, and updates the library after transaction success.
Delete requires explicit project-name confirmation; Cancel changes nothing and Confirm removes only the selected project and declared snapshots.
A semantic edit changes status from Saved to Unsaved then Saving without blocking continued editing.
One atomic IndexedDB transaction advances revision, retains the prior known-good snapshot, and changes status to Saved only after read-back.
Browser reload restores exact project ID, recipe, theme, preview state, revision, and render hash.
Storage view reports IndexedDB location, persistence result, approximate usage and quota, latest revision, and snapshot time without promising cloud backup.
Export downloads one .spriteproject archive with versioned manifest, project, pack locks, provenance, owned imports, and verified checksums.
Import validates entries, expansion bounds, checksums, schema versions, project summary, and required packs before enabling commit.
An existing project ID requires Replace, Import as copy, or Cancel; no option is precommitted before user activation.
After browser project storage is cleared, import restores exact semantic data, render hash, credits, and export metadata.
After one online load, an offline restart opens the cached app shell and visibly reports Offline.
An installed project and all installed pack content open and render with zero failed required network requests.
Generic and Godot downloads complete offline with the same hashes as the online project.
The extracted Windows x64 executable launches without installation or administrator rights and identifies its version.
Host status identifies Desktop and exposes folder actions while the renderer has no direct Node.js globals.
Recent list shows valid user-opened project folders; selecting a missing entry reports it and offers removal without creating files.
Create folder writes the canonical project structure only inside the user-selected empty or approved location.
Save writes a temporary validated document then atomically replaces the target; failure preserves the prior file and dirty state.
Open validates schema, pack locks, references, and external revision before exposing the project for editing.
Save As writes a complete new folder, preserves semantic project identity unless Copy is chosen, and leaves the source untouched.
Choose export folder displays the resolved user-selected destination and overwrite policy before writing.
Generic export writes exactly spritesheet, animations, build manifest, machine credits, and human credits with expected hashes.
Godot export adds valid SpriteFrames and instructions; a real pinned Godot fixture loads all declared animations without manual slicing.
Permission, conflict, or partial-write failure identifies the destination and leaves no result that appears complete.
Closing a dirty project blocks window destruction and presents Save, Discard, and Cancel with project name.
Cancel returns focus to the unchanged project and keeps the dirty state.
Save closes only after atomic persistence succeeds; save failure keeps the window open with recovery actions.
Discard closes without writing and the next open loads the last saved revision.
Web exports a valid archive whose manifest names the baseline schema, packs, checksums, and no browser-specific state.
Electron opens the web archive after validation and reports exact project, pack, migration, and source summary.
Desktop render hash, selected assets, resolved tokens, credits, animation JSON, and Godot metadata equal the web source.
Electron exports a portable archive without absolute paths, permission handles, recent entries, or host-only settings.
Web validates and imports the desktop archive transactionally with explicit conflict handling.
Web render hash, selected assets, resolved tokens, credits, animation JSON, and Godot metadata equal the desktop source.
Storage view states IndexedDB for web or the exact approved project folder for desktop and explicitly says no cloud backup is active.
Backup action produces a user-owned archive and reports completion only after its bytes and checksums are generated.
Creating, editing, saving, backing up, and exporting project data causes zero undeclared project-data network requests.

flows.completion: Browser project library manages several projects without cross-project mutation
Semantic edit persists transactionally and restores exactly
Archive restores the project after browser project storage is cleared

preconditions: App and selected packs loaded online once
flows.completion: Installed project workflow and exports complete offline
Desktop host opens with correct capabilities and recent-folder state
Project folder creates, saves, opens, and relocates without semantic drift
Generic and Godot outputs are written safely to selected directory
Save, Discard, and Cancel each produce the exact selected outcome
Electron continues the web project with exact parity
Web continues the desktop project with exact parity

preconditions: Project open on web or desktop
flows.completion: User sees storage location and owns a portable backup with no undeclared transfer

steps.action: Open Storage and inspect data location
Complete local edit save and export

exclusions.reason: UPD-001 provides manual archive portability without adding a funded backend
exclusions.disposition: Future backend update after accepted customer promise and operating plan

exclusions.expectation: Share or co-edit projects in real time
exclusions.reason: Collaboration requires identity, coordination, retention, and conflict services outside the zero-server boundary

exclusions.expectation: Continuously watch and update a running Godot project
exclusions.reason: This update establishes safe direct export folders but does not yet specify watcher lifecycle or engine coordination
exclusions.consequence: User runs direct export again after edits
exclusions.disposition: Future desktop expansion through formal update

exclusions.expectation: Launch or control Godot and other local tools
exclusions.reason: Process execution expands security and lifecycle scope beyond convenient file operations
Portable GitHub Release artifacts avoid an update service and signing dependency in this increment
exclusions.consequence: User downloads a newer portable ZIP manually
exclusions.disposition: Revisit with signing and distribution plan

exclusions.expectation: Install Electron through MSI, Store, or system package
exclusions.reason: The accepted promise is a portable app requiring no installation
exclusions.consequence: No file associations, Start menu registration, or automatic uninstall

exclusions.expectation: Provide portable macOS and Linux desktop builds
exclusions.reason: Windows x64 is the first evidence surface and avoids claiming untested packaging and signing
exclusions.consequence: macOS and Linux users use the web version
exclusions.disposition: Add only through host-specific verified update

exclusions.expectation: Provide a trusted signed Windows binary
exclusions.reason: Code-signing cost is not currently funded
exclusions.consequence: Portable launch may show an accurate SmartScreen warning

exclusions.reason: UPD-001 changes host and persistence, not pack authoring or watcher behavior
exclusions.consequence: Both hosts use bundled or formally installed pack data

exclusions.expectation: Allow web and desktop projects or rendering rules to diverge
exclusions.reason: Two products would destroy portability and duplicate validation
exclusions.consequence: Host-specific state remains outside canonical project data
exclusions.disposition: Permanently rejected unless a future ADR supersedes one-product-core principle

workPackages.gate: Update envelope, impact analysis, ADR-002, traceability, exclusions, and release gate pass review
Domain modules have no browser or Electron dependency and host contract tests pass
IndexedDB library, revisions, snapshots, migration, and failure recovery pass actual-UI flows
Archive validation, conflicts, migration, malicious fixtures, and exact round trip pass
Published-subpath PWA completes declared flows with network disconnected
Packaged portable Windows app launches with secure renderer and typed IPC checks
Folder lifecycle, atomic writes, failures, direct exports, and unsaved close pass packaged Electron UI flows
Bidirectional archive and output parity pass from real web and packaged Electron UIs
All affected RUN-MVP-001 baseline flows pass on affected hosts with zero severity 0-2 issues

decisions.decision: Keep GitHub Pages as the complete required web host and add portable Electron as an optional local-file host over one shared product core.
decisions.evidence: Owner direction and ADR-002; implementation evidence pending.

decisions.decision: Use IndexedDB for project records and snapshots and localStorage only for small preferences, pointers, and migration markers.
decisions.evidence: ADR-001 storage contract; migration and actual-UI evidence pending.

decisions.decision: Distribute the first Electron host as a portable Windows x64 ZIP through GitHub Releases without installer or automatic updates.
decisions.evidence: Zero-server and zero-signing-cost constraints; packaged validation pending.

decisions.decision: Canonical project, pack, provenance, and export schemas remain host-neutral; host-only state is stored separately.
decisions.evidence: Required to satisfy cross-host portability promise; parity evidence pending.
